name: Sun Dried Tomato Dip
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Sun-Dried Tomatoes, Dry (Not In Oil)
recipeInstructions:
MICROWAVE OVEN METHOD:
1. Combine tomatoes, water, garlic, oregano, and thyme in a 13x9x2-inch oval glass or ceramic dish.
2. Cover tightly with microwave plastic wrap.
3. Cook at 100% in a high-wattage oven for 8 minutes.
4. Prick the plastic to release steam. Remove from oven and uncover.
5. Stir well. Re-cover and cook for another 8 minutes.
6. Prick the plastic to release steam again. Remove from oven and uncover.
7. Stir well until cool and most of the liquid has been absorbed. Transfer to a 1-1/2-quart souffle dish.
8. Pour 3/4 cup of olive oil and vegetable oil over the tomato mixture.
9. Cover tightly with microwave plastic wrap and cook for 5 minutes.
10. Prick the plastic to release steam. Remove from oven and uncover.
11. Stir and allow to stand until cool. Transfer to a food processor bowl and puree until smooth.
12. Scrape into a large bowl and stir in any remaining olive oil if serving immediately. Otherwise, pack in a refrigerator storage container with a tightly fitting lid. The dip will keep for up to a month.
STOVE-TOP METHOD:
1. Cover sun-dried tomatoes with water in a nonreactive saucepan.
2. Bring to a boil over medium heat, about 8 minutes.
3. Reduce heat to a simmer and cook, covered, for 22 minutes.
4. Allow to cool. Drain the mixture.
5. Cook with oil, garlic, oregano, and thyme over low heat, covered, for 20 minutes.
6. Puree the mixture.
Above makes 3 cups.
